Selection Process The selection process for the Building Management System (BMS) Analytics Challenge consists of the following stages: 1. Data Cleaning and Preprocessing: In this stage, participants will be required to clean and preprocess the dataset to ensure data quality and consistency. This may include handling missing values, outliers, and inconsistencies, as well as converting timestamps into meaningful time-based features. 2. Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA): Participants will perform EDA to gain insights into the dataset and identify trends, patterns, and correlations. This may involve data visualization techniques, statistical analysis, and hypothesis testing. 3. Clustering Analysis (if applicable): Participants may choose to apply clustering techniques to segment the data into meaningful groups based on similarities. 4. Time Series Analysis (if applicable): Participants may conduct time series analysis to identify trends and forecast maintenance needs. 5. Predictive Modelling: Participants may develop predictive models to forecast maintenance needs or identify potential issues before they occur. 6. Dashboard Design and Interactivity: Participants will design and build an interactive dashboard using Google Data Studio (Looker’s Studio) to visualize key insights from their analysis. 7. Submission: Participants will submit their EDA report, interactive dashboard, and predictions for the test dataset. 8. Evaluation: Submissions will be evaluated based on the following criteria: - Data Cleaning & Preprocessing (20%) - Exploratory Data Analysis (40%) - Predictive Modelling (10%) - Dashboard Design & Interactivity (30%) The evaluation criteria weightages may vary slightly depending on the specific challenge requirements.

SELECTION PROCESS Our selection process is designed to ensure that we find the best talent for our teams. It consists of the following stages: 1. Application: Candidates submit their applications through our website or career fairs. 2. Eligibility Check: We review applications to ensure candidates meet the minimum eligibility requirements. 3. Online Test: Candidates take a 90-minute online test, which includes multiple-choice questions, coding tasks, and a SQL query. 4. Interview: Selected candidates are invited for a face-to-face interview with a hiring manager or team. 5. Offer: Successful candidates receive a job offer, which includes a salary and benefits package.

SELECTION PROCESS We follow a rigorous selection process to ensure that we find the best talent for our teams. Our process includes several rounds, each designed to assess different skills and competencies. Round 1: Application Review In the first round, we review applications to ensure that candidates meet the minimum eligibility criteria. This includes checking academic records, projects, and work experience. Round 2: Technical Assessment The second round is a technical assessment, where candidates are evaluated on their problem-solving abilities, analytical thinking, and coding skills. This round may include coding challenges, data analysis tasks, or algorithm design questions. Round 3: Interview The third round is a face-to-face or virtual interview with a team member or hiring manager. This round is designed to assess communication skills, cultural fit, and motivation. Candidates may be asked behavioral questions, technical questions, or may be asked to present a project or solution. Round 4: Background Check The final round is a background check, where we verify educational qualifications, work history, and criminal records. This round is essential to ensure that we hire candidates who meet our high standards of integrity and trustworthiness. We believe that our selection process is fair, transparent, and merit-based, and we are committed to providing equal opportunities to all candidates. ---------------- SELECTION PROCESS

SELECTION PROCESS The eligibility criteria for the Software Engineer Intern position include having a B.E./ B.Tech./ Computer Science/ Information Technology / Electronics/Electrical degree with a minimum of 70% or 7 CGPA. The candidates must also have good programming skills, preferably in C/ C++/ C#/ Python, and excellent communication skills. The selection process for the Software Engineer Intern position involves an online written test in Data Science Lab Innovation center, which is a video-only test with no audio. The test consists of two coding questions, both of medium level. The candidates who pass this test will be considered eligible for the interview round. The interview round will be conducted in the form of a coding test, which allows the use of C#/C++/C and Python. The internship duration is from July 2025 to December 2025, with the possibility of extension until June 2026 based on intern performance and business needs. The candidates who perform exceptionally in the evaluation process may be offered a PPO (Pre-Placement Offer) along with the internship, while others will be evaluated during the internship period for performance. Full-time positions, if offered, will be based on business needs and student performance during the internship. The full-time CTC (Cost to Company) is 15.381 LPA, and additional benefits for full-time roles are not applicable for the internship. The candidates must opt-in for the internship and apply before 27th February 2025, 10:00 AM. The start date for the internship will be announced later.

Selection Process The selection process for the role of Hardware Engineer involves multiple rounds. In the first round, candidates are evaluated based on their academic records and technical skills through a written test or coding assessment. Candidates who perform well in this round are invited for interviews. The interview process typically involves a series of technical interviews with different teams. These interviews may focus on specific areas of hardware design, such as logic design, RTL coding, verification, and physical design. Candidates may also be asked about their experience and motivation for the role. The final selection is based on the candidate's performance in the interviews and their overall fit with the team and the organization. Candidates who are selected are offered a position and a compensation package. For the role of Software Engineer, the selection process is similar but may involve different rounds and assessments. Candidates may be evaluated based on their academic records, technical skills, and experience. They may be asked to participate in coding challenges or design exercises during the interview process. The final selection is based on the candidate's technical skills, problem-solving abilities, and cultural fit with the team and the organization. The eligibility criteria for both roles include a Master's degree with a minimum CGPA of 7. Candidates are not subjected to direct resume screening, but are shortlisted based on their academic records and invited for interviews. The selection process is competitive, with multiple teams interviewing candidates for each role. Ultimately, one candidate is selected for each role and offered an internship or full-time position.

The candidate will be assessed based on their academic background, programming skills, understanding of operating systems and algorithms, and problem-solving abilities. The selection process includes an online test, attendance profile and CTC round, and a system software engineering intern round. The stipend for the internship is 80T PM. Selection Process The selection process for the graduate intern role at SAP Labs consists of the following rounds: 1. Online Test: This is a mandatory round for all applicants. It is a 45-minute, SAP-based, written test that can be taken remotely. The test includes two coding questions of easy level. 2. Technical Interview: This round is based on a panel interview. Candidates who clear the online test are invited for this round. 3. Managerial Interview: This round assesses a candidate's managerial skills and potential. Only those who clear the technical interview are invited for this round. 4. Final Round: The final round is a one-on-one interview with a hiring manager. Only one candidate is selected for the role in each round. The eligibility criteria for the graduate intern role at SAP Labs are: - A minimum of 70% or 7 CGPA throughout the academic career. - Applicants must apply before the last date and time for their preferred location. The role details for the graduate intern role are: - Role: Graduate Intern - Designation: Core I (Spot) - Stipend: INR 35,000 per month - Duration: 12 months - Start Date: To be announced The application deadline for the graduate intern role is 27th February 2025, 11:00 AM. Online Test 2. Technical Interview 3. Managerial Interview 4. Final Round The online test is a mandatory round for all applicants and consists of two coding questions of easy level. The technical interview is based on a panel interview and is for those who clear the online test. The managerial interview assesses a candidate's managerial skills and potential, and only those who clear the technical interview are invited for this round. The final round is a one-on-one interview with a hiring manager, and only one candidate is selected for the role in each round.

Selection Process: Objectives: - Understand the behavior of EM waves, on interaction with periodic structures (e.g.: surface roughness, pyramid structure etc.) of different length scale. - The behavior of the EM waves on different surfaces can change with varying frequencies. The physics governing these behavior needs to be understood, characterized, and implemented in the modelling. - Develop & validate analytical and simulation methods to predict the system level performance with these features for specified application. Work Scope: - Thorough research to understand the physics of wave interaction with different surfaces features. - Perform literature search, benchmarking, and baseline studies/analysis. - Learn the predictive tools required for the project. - Develop the predictive models to understand and evaluate the effect of these surface features for specified applications (Eg: ADAS Radome/Absorber, Antennas etc.) - Work with team members to prototype and test the design concept. - Validate the CAE model with the test results. - Documentation and reporting. Please select the type of internship - Engineering interns - 6 to 10 Months Assigned project lead/mentor for the intern - Priyanka Bhagawath

Final Interview The Online Written Test will be conducted online and will consist of multiple-choice questions and coding problems. The duration of the test will be 2 hours. The Technical Interview will be conducted by the technical team and will focus on the candidate's technical skills and knowledge. The Final Interview will be conducted by the hiring manager and will be the final round of the selection process. The selection process may vary slightly depending on the specific requirements of the role and the organization.
